topic: oil change and tire rotation — keeping the car alive

turns.content: yo quick question. how often should you rotate tires
Generally every 5,000 to 7,500 miles, or roughly every time you get an oil change. Are you due for one?
yeah man i drive a lot so i lose track. oil light been on for like a week too lol
A week with the oil light on is definitely something to take care of ASAP. Driving on old or low oil can cause serious engine damage. Are you able to get it into a shop soon?
going tomorrow morning before i start driving. theres a jiffy lube near me. they do oil change and tire rotation together right
Yep, Jiffy Lube does both. They usually have combo deals too — oil change plus tire rotation for a set price. Might be worth asking about that when you get there to save a few bucks.
how much should that run me. i dont wanna get finessed

For a standard oil change plus tire rotation, you're usually looking at around $60-$90 total. If they try to push synthetic oil on you, that bumps it up. Check if your car actually needs synthetic — most older cars do fine with conventional.
lol they always try to sell you the air filter bro every single time. ill do conventional thats fine. just need to keep this car alive as long as possible
Ha, it's their classic move. Conventional is totally fine for most cars. One tip — keep track of your mileage when you get the oil changed so you know when the next one is due.
yeah thats facts. do i need to worry about the tires themselves tho like the tread and all that

Good question. You can do a quick check with a penny — stick it in the tread with Lincoln's head facing down. If you can see the top of his head, your tread is too low and you need new tires. They'll also usually tell you at the shop if they notice anything.
what about tire pressure tho. like i see the light come on sometimes when it gets cold but then it goes away

That's normal — cold air makes the pressure drop a little, so the sensor triggers. But it's still worth checking the actual pressure with a gauge when it happens. Most gas stations have free air pumps, and the right PSI for your tires is usually on a sticker inside the driver's door.
oh word i never knew about the sticker thing. good looking out bro
Yeah most people don't notice it! It tells you the exact pressure for front and rear tires. Little things like that keep your car running longer.
ok bet ima do that before i go. appreciate it bro
No problem! Good luck tomorrow, get that oil light taken care of.
